failure_surface: Agent failed to correctly read and process cell values from cells containing Excel formulas, resulting in ArrayFormula objects being used in comparisons and sorting operations instead of actual computed values.
root_cause: The get_cell_value() helper function did not properly detect and handle openpyxl's ArrayFormula object type. When cells contain Excel formulas (e.g., DENSE.RANK.EQ), openpyxl returns special formula objects rather than the computed result. Comparing these objects during sorting caused TypeError, and storing them in the analysis dictionary led to incorrect ranking results where all items were ranked as 1 due to failed class grouping.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before extracting and comparing cell values, explicitly check if the cell.value is an instance of openpyxl.formula.ArrayFormula (or similar formula wrapper types) and either skip those rows, use the .value attribute properly, or load the workbook with calculated values mode enabled.
evidence: First execution threw: TypeError: '<' not supported between instances of 'ArrayFormula' and 'ArrayFormula' when sorting
Second execution threw: TypeError: unsupported format string passed to ArrayFormula.__format__ when printing verification
Final verification output showed: Class=<openpyxl.worksheet.formula.ArrayFormula object at 0x...> instead of actual class string values
Final summary showed all 288 ranks equal to 1, indicating failed grouping logic due to unparseable class values
Despite having 288 valid entries, the Unique Item Classes count was 288, suggesting no proper string extraction occurred

memory_items.title: Handle Formula Cells During Data Extraction
memory_items.lesson: When reading cells from Excel files using openpyxl, cells may contain formula objects (ArrayFormula, CellFormula) rather than computed values. These must be detected and handled before comparison or arithmetic operations.
memory_items.generalization: Always check isinstance(cell.value, openpyxl.formula.ArrayFormula) or similar formula types before treating cell.value as a primitive. Consider loading workbooks with data_only=True option or implementing explicit formula detection in value extraction helpers.

memory_items.title: Validate Cell Values Before Sorting Operations
memory_items.lesson: Sorting or grouping operations on spreadsheet data require consistent primitive types. Formula objects cannot be compared or serialized like strings or numbers.
memory_items.generalization: Implement defensive data extraction that converts all cell contents to appropriate Python primitives (str, int, float) before building lists for sorting, filtering, or aggregation. Catch and log unexpected types for debugging.

memory_items.title: Verify Extracted Values Match Expected Format
memory_items.lesson: Debug prints showing object representations (e.g., <openpyxl.worksheet.formula.ArrayFormula object>) indicate failed value extraction, even if code runs without crashing.
memory_items.generalization: After extracting cell values for computation, include sanity checks in verification steps that confirm extracted values are the expected type (str for categories, numbers for amounts). Fail fast on type mismatches rather than proceeding with invalid data.
